title: Information on Enterprise plans
excerpt:
Can anyone provide me any insight into enterprise plans? 
I’ve submitted a request for information at least 3 times over the past 2 months but apparently whoever receives those requests doesn’t feel I’m worth responding …

posts.author: joshuams (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can anyone provide me any insight into enterprise plans?

I’ve submitted a request for information at least 3 times over the past 2 months but apparently whoever receives those requests doesn’t feel I’m worth responding to.

I’m particularly interested in the being able to restrict permissions. We’d like to be able to have each partner to have their own private workspace, but also allowing admins access to them.

Can we accomplish this without being on the enterprise plan? If enterprise is our only option, could anyone give me an idea of what pricing would look like for a company of < 10 people?

posts.author: loovanloon (Ton)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Reluctant over there at Monday, I’ll try next Monday…again.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @joshuams - normally the absolute minimum for ENT plans is 15 users, so if you are looking for less than 10 it is likely not going to be approved.

As for pricing, you can count on almost double the price of PRO licensing.

If you want to learn more about ENT licensing feel free to book a discovery session with us and we can walk you through the enhanced permissions to see if it is the right fit for you. Also, sometimes there are design workarounds that can make it work in PRO.

Book A Discovery Session - Green Llamas Consulting

Thanks!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: Eduardo (EDUARDO ESCOBEDO)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 We need the same thing but we are a small team.

posts.author: ramnarayanank (Ram Narayanan)
posts.cookedHtml:
From a technical standpoint, the kind of permission control you are describing isolated partner workspaces with centralized admin oversight is typically implemented via advanced role-based access control (RBAC) and workspace level segmentation. These features are often part of enterprise plans due to the complexity of data governance and compliance requirements.

In CONTUS Tech’s enterprise application development practices such setups leverage multi tenant architecture and granular ACLs which are rarely supported in lower tier plans. Pricing can vary widely based on customization, security needs and integration scope.
